headline: Survey at 32% completion with strong teacher engagement but uneven regional coverage.
collection_status: 434 responses collected against 1,350 target (32% complete). Lindi and Mara dominate with 167 of 434 responses (38%), while Rukwa contributes only 12 (3%). Current pace suggests 3–4 months needed to reach target if momentum holds.
key_findings: Teacher participation robust: 174 lower-primary and 59 upper-primary KF teachers comprise 53% of sample, plus 103 head teachers ensure school-level perspective.
Expansion dominates priorities: 155 respondents (36%) prioritise expanding KF vs 73 (17%) for strengthening existing work, signalling appetite for scale over consolidation.
Scale readiness gap critical: Average 2.7/5 readiness score contrasts sharply with 4.4/5 partner satisfaction, indicating stakeholders want growth but doubt operational capacity.
Policy integration emerging: 89 responses (20%) emphasize policy alignment and government collaboration, reflected in Swahili feedback on embedding KF in national education strategy.
flags: Regional imbalance: Rukwa, Songwe, and Singida underrepresented (<30 responses each); risk incomplete picture of northern/western needs.
Readiness-ambition mismatch: Scale readiness (2.7) insufficient to deliver expansion priority (155 votes); operational bottlenecks likely unaddressed.
Low senior official voices: Only 8 regional/district education officers (RC, DEO, ARC, REO, DRO combined) vs 174 teachers; decision-maker perspective thin.
recommendation: Immediately intensify collection in underrepresented regions (target 50+ responses each in Rukwa, Songwe, Singida) and deliberately sample 15–20 additional district/regional officials to validate scale readiness constraints. Use next 4 weeks to close the regional-leadership gap before final analysis.
